name: Potage St. Germain
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the drained dried peas in the soup kettle.
2. Add chicken broth; bring to a boil.
3. Simmer on low for 10 minutes. Skim any froth from the top of the soup.
4. Melt butter in a skillet and sauté carrots and onions until onions are golden brown.
5. Add leeks and lettuce; simmer on low for an additional 10 minutes.
6. Stir the vegetable mixture into the soup, then add sugar and salt. Simmer until split peas are tender, about 2 hours.
7. Puree the fresh peas using a sieve or potato masher.
8. Force the soup mixture through a sieve as well.
9. Return the pureed mixture to the kettle.
10. Stir in cream and milk. Heat gently on low, stirring occasionally to prevent scorching.
11. Just before serving, add butter.
12. Garnish with a dollop of sour cream, a dash of sherry, or both.
